design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Principal surrogate paradox phase threshold. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are Frangakis-Rubin principal stratification and surrogate-paradox results such as Chen-Geng-style causal necessity/sufficiency criteria. Why non-trivial? The target is not another Manski principal-strata LP bound: require a finite binary principal-strata witness class with an explicit sign-reversal threshold where the treatment effect on the surrogate is positive in every observable arm but the principal-stratum effect on the outcome flips sign. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a hand-checkable phase threshold plus endpoint-attaining witness family. If the delta reduces to missing-cell bounds, LP duality, or definition-unfold surrogate criteria, pivot or accept field-tier.

proposed_from.topic: Principal surrogate paradox phase threshold. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are Frangakis-Rubin principal stratification and surrogate-paradox results such as Chen-Geng-style causal necessity/sufficiency criteria. Why non-trivial? The target is not another Manski principal-strata LP bound: require a finite binary principal-strata witness class with an explicit sign-reversal threshold where the treatment effect on the surrogate is positive in every observable arm but the principal-stratum effect on the outcome flips sign. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a hand-checkable phase threshold plus endpoint-attaining witness family. If the delta reduces to missing-cell bounds, LP duality, or definition-unfold surrogate criteria, pivot or accept field-tier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains failed pid_long_term_surrogate_v1, candidate pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1, and downgraded pid_collider_bias_sharp_v1. The v3 delta is a binary Frangakis-Rubin responder-stratum phase frontier with explicit endpoint tables; it is not the rejected prevalence-only phase diagram or an ordinal K>=3 compatibility facet.","published_axis":"The v3 text no longer claims to improve YinLiuGengLuo2020 as an exclusion theorem. The published-axis gap is inclusion-side: when average criteria or observed-data exclusion criteria do not rule out a surrogate paradox, Conjecture 1 asks for the finite responder-stratum witnesses and the phase threshold gamma_star=1-pi_R."}
seed_list: Introduce a closed-form responder-stratum frontier T_R(P) for binary monotone principal surrogates and conjecture it is necessary and sufficient for positive-observable, negative-responder surrogate-paradox witnesses.
Derive observed-table algebraic inequalities deciding whether two binary P(S,Y|Z) tables admit a principal-surrogate paradox witness without reducing to a generic Manski LP.
Classify monotonicity, causal necessity, and no-harm restrictions into impossible, shifted-threshold, and observationally-hidden surrogate-paradox regimes.
Show average causal sufficiency has a vacuous worst-responder lower bound by an endpoint-attaining finite binary principal-strata witness.
Use shared principal-stratum compatibility facets to prove a binary surrogate-paradox frontier strictly sharper than pairwise cell bounds.
Construct an open class of binary principal-surrogate laws where positive CEP-surface slices coexist with a negative responder effect.
Build a covariate-indexed responder frontier T_R(P_x) and characterize when conditional positivity can mask an unconditional surrogate paradox.
Bridge Yin-Liu-Geng-Luo exclusion criteria with a constructive inclusion frontier by proving complementarity in the binary monotone model.
Give a minimal three-stratum witness proving that individual surrogate paradox phenomena persist after imposing average causal necessity.
Define a non-LP coupling representation of the binary principal-surrogate identified set and compare it to Kuroki-Cai sharp bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce a closed-form responder-stratum frontier T_R(P) for binary monotone principal surrogates and conjecture it is necessary and sufficient for positive-observable, negative-responder surrogate-paradox witnesses.

anchor_paper.author: Frangakis and Rubin; Chen, Geng, and Jia
anchor_paper.claim: Principal strata define causal subpopulations, while surrogate-paradox criteria are average endpoint sign criteria.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Principal effects are causal effects within a principal stratum; treatment has a positive effect on the surrogate...negative effect.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/phase-threshold pattern; stronger because it names T_R(P), endpoint witnesses, and positive observed concordance.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from binary mixture bounds because monotonicity reveals the responder prevalence.
seed_details.rebuttal: Prevalence is revealed, but the positive-concordance constrained sign frontier and endpoint-attaining common law are not in the comparator papers.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ChenGengJia2007 because it gives an inclusion frontier for theta_R, not an average consistency criterion.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine mixture inversion creates the named scalar T_R(P), a witness class, and an iff phase claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: Threshold could read as a two-cell missing-data bound.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the witness also enforces Delta_S>0 and c_0,c_1>0 in a common principal law.

adversarial_referee.instance: The draft might prove only T_R(P)>0 sufficient.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Conjecture 1 explicitly requires necessity and endpoint equality at T_R(P)=0.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single tuned table may drive the effect.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Conjecture 2 promotes the exhibit to a relatively open class.

seed_details.burned_reason: D-0.5 reviews found field-tier definitional endpoint algebra, missing/nonverified witnesses, bad comparator/citation anchors, and not-publishable finite-table claims across angles 0-3.

seed_details.one_line: Derive observed-table algebraic inequalities deciding whether two binary P(S,Y|Z) tables admit a principal-surrogate paradox witness without reducing to a generic Manski LP.

anchor_paper.author: VanderWeele; Kuroki and Cai; Yin, Liu, Geng, and Luo
anchor_paper.claim: Observed favorable surrogate relations do not settle sign reversal; bounds and exclusion criteria exist.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: positive treatment-surrogate and surrogate-outcome relations do not rule out the surrogate paradox

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP risk; stronger if final inequalities are closed-form and non-LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: KurokiCai2013 already gives sharp binary surrogate bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed targets inverse feasibility under positive concordance and a named responder-stratum sign, not generic endpoint bounds.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not KurokiCai2013 because the deliverable is a closed-form paradox-inclusion frontier for theta_R.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if inequalities avoid a black-box LP and produce endpoint tables; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: Yin et al. may already characterize the complement.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal requires checking their criteria; seed kept as backup, not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: No focal object if only inequalities listed.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: name the feasibility cone and its support-function frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Kuroki-Cai may target a different estimand.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: position as different target, not contradiction.

seed_details.burned_reason: D-0.5 reviews found field-tier definitional endpoint algebra, missing/nonverified witnesses, bad comparator/citation anchors, and not-publishable finite-table claims across angles 0-3.

seed_details.one_line: Classify monotonicity, causal necessity, and no-harm restrictions into impossible, shifted-threshold, and observationally-hidden surrogate-paradox regimes.

anchor_paper.author: Gilbert and Hudgens; Ma, Yin, Liu, and Geng; Ding and Lu
anchor_paper.claim: Principal-surrogate predictiveness and individual paradoxes leave assumption-sensitive threshold regimes open.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: causal effect predictiveness (CEP) surface; individual surrogate paradox

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption classification; stronger if assumptions shift a named T_R(P), weaker if only sufficient conditions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a taxonomy of known assumptions.
seed_details.rebuttal: The trichotomy would compute how each assumption moves or destroys the same threshold object.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not GilbertHudgens2008 because it classifies sign-frontier regimes rather than defining CEP.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with explicit threshold formulas per regime.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could give sufficient no-paradox assumptions only.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: family statement can include N,R,A cell effects.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computed delta if assumptions are qualitative.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: threshold shift is the focal scalar.

seed_details.burned_reason: D-0.5 reviews found field-tier definitional endpoint algebra, missing/nonverified witnesses, bad comparator/citation anchors, and not-publishable finite-table claims across angles 0-3.

seed_details.one_line: Show average causal sufficiency has a vacuous worst-responder lower bound by an endpoint-attaining finite binary principal-strata witness.

anchor_paper.author: Chen, Geng, and Jia; Frangakis and Rubin; Ma, Yin, Liu, and Geng
anchor_paper.claim: Average sufficiency does not directly control worst principal-stratum signs.
anchor_paper.location: ChenGengJia2007 abstract; prior pid_long_term_surrogate review angle2_v1
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: The point-ID phase diagram is algebra from the dilation interval

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample pattern; stronger only if endpoint-attaining over a nondegenerate open class.
seed_details.referee_objection: A vacuity example is immediate by heterogeneity.
seed_details.rebuttal: The finite witness must preserve average sufficiency and positive observed concordance while driving the worst responder effect.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ChenGengJia2007 because it asks for a worst-stratum lower bound, not average consistency.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless promoted to an open-class theorem.
seed_details.burned_reason: D-0.5 reviews found field-tier definitional endpoint algebra, missing/nonverified witnesses, bad comparator/citation anchors, and not-publishable finite-table claims across angles 0-3.

seed_details.one_line: Use shared principal-stratum compatibility facets to prove a binary surrogate-paradox frontier strictly sharper than pairwise cell bounds.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Shared principal strata can create compatibility facets beyond pairwise relaxations.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the shared middle stratum creates compatibility facets

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP risk; stronger if facet gives a named strict-containment witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: Ordinal K>=3 compatibility does not imply binary surrogate compatibility.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed would prove the binary analogue directly with the responder stratum shared across both observed mixtures.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not pid_ordinal_principal_strata because K=2 surrogate endpoints use sign reversal, not ordinal adjacent-chain geometry.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with strict pairwise-vs-common-law containment witness.

adversarial_referee.instance: Prior proposal may already contain the compatibility idea.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: current endpoint is binary surrogate paradox, not ordinal K>=3 sensitivity.

adversarial_referee.instance: Strict containment could rely on a corner table.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: focal object is the compatibility facet inequality.

seed_details.one_line: Construct an open class of binary principal-surrogate laws where positive CEP-surface slices coexist with a negative responder effect.

anchor_paper.author: Gilbert and Hudgens
anchor_paper.claim: CEP surface evaluates principal surrogate endpoints but is not reduced to a binary sign phase diagram.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: We introduce an estimand for evaluating a principal surrogate, the causal effect predictiveness (CEP) surface

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Phenomenon-anchor; stronger if promoted from one witness to an open class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a restatement of CEP heterogeneity.
seed_details.rebuttal: CEP defines the surface; the seed gives an observable-table open class with sign-reversal witnesses.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not GilbertHudgens2008 because it gives constructive binary inclusion conditions for a negative responder effect.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if open-class proof is included; single example downgrades.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could be one hand-tuned CEP surface.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: responder is the named focal stratum because monotonicity identifies its prevalence.
Rebuttal: pair with T_R(P) from the chosen seed.

seed_details.one_line: Build a covariate-indexed responder frontier T_R(P_x) and characterize when conditional positivity can mask an unconditional surrogate paradox.

anchor_paper.author: Ding and Lu
anchor_paper.claim: Principal scores make covariate-indexed principal strata central to identification.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: whenever the treatment has no effect on the surrogate, it has no effect on the outcome

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: CATE version; weaker unless conditional frontiers reveal an aggregation sign gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine covariate stratification of the binary formula.
seed_details.rebuttal: Could be novel only if aggregation creates sign reversal absent in every x-cell; not chosen.

seed_details.one_line: Bridge Yin-Liu-Geng-Luo exclusion criteria with a constructive inclusion frontier by proving complementarity in the binary monotone model.

anchor_paper.author: Yin, Liu, Geng, and Luo
anchor_paper.claim: Optimal criteria exclude surrogate paradoxes, but constructive inclusion threshold witnesses are not the stated target.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: we provide novel criteria to exclude the surrogate paradox

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge; stronger if complementarity is exact, weaker if just adjacent criteria.
seed_details.referee_objection: The complement of their exclusion criterion may already be the inclusion set.
seed_details.rebuttal: Need verify; if their complement lacks endpoint-attaining principal-stratum witnesses, gap survives.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not YinLiuGengLuo2019 if it constructs the latent responder witness absent from exclusion criteria.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only after exact complement check.

adversarial_referee.instance: Closest paper may close the complement already.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; downgrade unless deep scan confirms a witness gap.

adversarial_referee.instance: May confuse average endpoint and responder-stratum effects.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: focal object is constructive inclusion frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Give a minimal three-stratum witness proving that individual surrogate paradox phenomena persist after imposing average causal necessity.

anchor_paper.author: Ma, Yin, Liu, and Geng
anchor_paper.claim: Individual surrogate paradoxes show average criteria can miss unit-level or stratum-level failures.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: average surrogate validity can miss individual or stratum-level sign failures

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample; weaker because it may be an example rather than a theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: A minimal witness is too small for flagship novelty.
seed_details.rebuttal: Kept as field-tier exhibit support for the main frontier, not as flagship headline.

seed_details.one_line: Define a non-LP coupling representation of the binary principal-surrogate identified set and compare it to Kuroki-Cai sharp bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Kuroki and Cai
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp surrogate endpoint bounds motivate a non-LP coupling form for the principal-strata sign set.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: sharp bounds on causal effects using a surrogate endpoint

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP; stronger if coupling representation is evaluable and not just LP duality.
seed_details.referee_objection: Binary finite-support coupling is equivalent to an LP.
seed_details.rebuttal: The only possible delta is interpretability/evaluability; not selected as primary.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not KurokiCai2013 only if the coupling yields a new closed-form sign frontier.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier if only LP duality.

proposed_from.literature_map: Frangakis and Rubin (2002, Biometrics, FrangakisRubin2002) introduce principal stratification and causal effects within strata defined by post-treatment potential values. Chen, Geng, and Jia (2007, JRSSB, ChenGengJia2007) define the surrogate paradox and consistency criteria at an average endpoint level. Gilbert and Hudgens (2008, Biometrics, GilbertHudgens2008) define the CEP surface for principal surrogate endpoints. VanderWeele (2013, Biometrics, VanderWeele2013) shows favorable surrogate associations do not by themselves exclude endpoint sign reversal. Kuroki and Cai (2013, Biometrics, KurokiCai2013) frame binary surrogate use as partial identification. Yin, Liu, Geng, and Luo (2019, SJS, YinLiuGengLuo2019) give optimal exclusion criteria, while the present angle asks for constructive inclusion and a threshold witness. Ma, Yin, Liu, and Geng (2021, Biostatistics, MaYinLiuGeng2021) sharpen the average-versus-individual gap. Ding and Lu (2017, JRSSB, DingLu2017) motivate monotone principal-strata conventions. Prior pid_long_term_surrogate was rejected as prevalence-interval algebra; pid_ordinal_principal_strata and pid_collider_bias_sharp are adjacent finite-strata threshold technologies but not binary surrogate-endpoint phase frontiers.
